reason: REPLACED (weakest position sold for new pick)

reasoning.upside: Takeover narrative is strengthening post-Apogee deal, and Viking's oral + injectable dual approach gives any acquirer a complete obesity franchise. Phase 3 data readouts in H2 2026 provide additional catalysts.
reasoning.risk: Clinical failure in Phase 3 would crater this 50%+; also, if Lilly/Novo resolve supply issues faster than expected, the urgency for big pharma to acquire diminishes.

reason: REPLACED (weakest position sold for new pick)

reasoning.upside: RSI at 1.46 is historically unprecedented oversold; mean reversion alone suggests 20-30% upside, and fundamental re-rating toward analyst targets ($64 median) provides the multi-week catalyst.
reasoning.risk: Franchise churn or retention issues could justify the discount; also, rising rates eventually slow homebuying activity (their core distribution channel).

reason: REPLACED (weakest position sold for new pick)

reasoning.upside: PE take-private reports are rarely leaked unless serious; insurance brokerages are among the most attractive PE targets given predictable cash flows and multiple expansion potential under private ownership.
reasoning.risk: Deal talks could collapse or never formalize — these "exploring strategic options" headlines sometimes lead nowhere, and the stock would retrace most of the 36% weekly gain.

reason: REPLACED (weakest position sold for new pick)

reasoning.upside: Priority Review designation (if granted) compresses the timeline to potential approval, and DMD/DM1 have limited competition — first-mover advantage in these indications is durable.
reasoning.risk: FDA could issue a Refuse to File letter or require additional data; also, manufacturing scale-up for gene therapies often causes unexpected delays.

reason: REPLACED (weakest position sold for new pick)

reasoning.upside: FDA-aligned NDA pathway removes the biggest regulatory overhang; Q4 submission gives a clear catalyst calendar and institutions tend to build positions ahead of submissions.
reasoning.risk: Wet AMD is competitive (Eylea, Vabysmo, Lucentis biosimilars) and AXPAXLI's sustained-release mechanism needs to demonstrate clear superiority on convenience to win share.
